title: Guajava oligosperma
taxonomy: Guajava oligosperma is classified as a species within the family Rubiaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as the madder or coffee family. Guajava oligosperma is closely related to other species in the genus Psidium, which includes the guava tree. The exact classification of Guajava oligosperma may vary depending on the source. It is considered to be a type of shrub or small tree native to certain regions.
uses: Guajava oligosperma, a plant native to certain regions, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. intro: Guajava oligosperma, also known as the small-fruited guava, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and protecting against various diseases.
